ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: Brothless mazemen—three forms of pork tossed with fresh ramen noodles in a savory roasted-garlic sauce, finished with pea tips and a sesame-nori spice blend.
items: 10 oz ground pork
1 slice bacon
¾ lb fresh ramen noodles
1 oz pea tips
1 bunch garlic chives
2 tbsp pork demi-glace
2 tbsp soy sauce
2 tbsp mirin
1 head garlic
1-inch piece fresh ginger
½ tsp Mazemen spice blend (black & white sesame, kibbled nori, smoked paprika)
Olive oil, salt & pepper
ingredients.callout: Mazemen = brothless ramen. The sauce is thick and clinging rather than soupy—make sure to drain the noodles very well before tossing them.

shoppingList.item: Sesame seeds, nori, smoked paprika (for spice blend)

body.html: A Blue Apron classic: brothless Japanese mazemen loaded with three forms of pork—ground pork, crispy bacon, and pork demi-glace—tossed with fresh ramen noodles in a deeply savory roasted-garlic sauce. Pea tips add freshness; a sesame-nori spice blend finishes the bowl.

items: Roast the garlic — whole head at 475°F, 28–30 min
Prep ingredients — dice bacon, mince ginger, slice chives
Cook the pork trio — bacon first, then aromatics, then ground pork
Build & simmer the sauce — squeeze in roasted garlic, add liquids
Boil & toss noodles — drain well, coat in sauce, stir in pea tips

body.html: Makes 3 servings. The recipe divides ⅔ into 2 bowls for dinner—you'll have one serving left over.

slides.title: Roast the Garlic
slides.checkboxLabel: Garlic roasted and set aside to cool

items: Preheat oven to 475°F.
Cut off and discard the top of the garlic head to expose the cloves, keeping the rest of the head intact.
Place on a piece of aluminum foil. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Tightly wrap in foil. Place on a sheet pan and roast 28–30 minutes, until cloves are browned and very tender.
Remove from oven, carefully unwrap, and set aside to cool slightly.

body.html: Why whole-head roasting? Slow heat turns sharp garlic mellow, sweet, and almost buttery—the flavor foundation of the sauce.

slides.title: Prepare the Ingredients
slides.checkboxLabel: All ingredients prepped and water boiling

body.html: While the garlic roasts, get everything else ready.

items: Wash and dry all fresh produce.
Heat a medium pot of salted water to boiling on high (for the noodles later).
Peel and mince the ginger.
Thinly slice the garlic chives.
Roughly chop any large pea tips.
Small dice the bacon.
In a large pot, heat 1 tsp olive oil on medium until hot.
Add the diced bacon and cook, stirring occasionally, 3–4 minutes, or until browned and crispy.
Do not drain—the rendered fat flavors the entire dish.

slides.title: Add the Aromatics & Ground Pork
slides.checkboxLabel: Pork cooked through and heat off

items: Add the minced ginger and all but a big pinch of the garlic chives to the pot of bacon.
Cook, stirring frequently, 2–3 minutes, until softened and fragrant.
Add the ground pork; season with salt and pepper.
Cook, frequently breaking the meat apart with a spoon, 4–5 minutes, until lightly browned and cooked through.
Season again with salt and pepper. Turn off the heat.
When the garlic is cool enough to handle, gently squeeze the individual cloves out of the head into the pot; discard the skins.
Add the soy sauce, mirin, demi-glace, and 1 cup water; stir to thoroughly combine.
Heat to boiling on high.
Reduce heat to medium; simmer, stirring occasionally, 2–3 minutes, until the liquid is slightly reduced in volume.
While the sauce simmers, add the noodles to the pot of boiling water, gently stirring to separate. Cook 2–3 minutes, until tender.
Drain thoroughly and transfer to the pot of sauce.
Cook, stirring constantly, 1–2 minutes, until the noodles are thoroughly coated.
Turn off the heat. Stir in the pea tips; season with salt and pepper to taste.
Divide ⅔ of the finished mazemen between 2 bowls (you'll have one serving extra).
Garnish with the remaining garlic chives and as much of the spice blend as you like.

body.html: Spice blend: Black sesame seeds, white sesame seeds, kibbled nori & smoked paprika—sprinkle generously for crunch and umami.
